title: Subset Sum Problem

content.problemStatement:
You have NNN positive integers, ithi^{th}ith of them being AiA_iAi​ and an positive integer XXX. 

You need to answer if you can pick a subset of these elements such that the sum of the subset is equal to xxx.

HintJust like the 0/1 knapsack problem, we have to pick or not pick the integers, but instead of maximising the value, we need to check if we can make the required sum or not.


Input Format

The first line contains a two integers NNN and XXX, denoting the length of array AAA and the target sum value.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
Output `YES` if you can pick a subset with sum equal to XXX, otherwise output `NO`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and X, the length of the array and the target sum. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if a subset of the given numbers sums to X, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: subset [1,4] gives 5 → YES. Test case 2: no subset sums to 8 → NO. Test case 3: subset [1,2,3,4] gives 10 → YES.
Target sum is 0, achievable by picking no elements.

solution.approach: The problem is the classic 0/1 subset‑sum decision problem. We maintain a boolean DP array dp[s] that tells whether a sum s can be obtained using some subset of the numbers processed so far. Initialise dp[0]=true (empty subset). For each element a we traverse sums from X down to a and set dp[s]|=dp[s‑a]. The downward traversal guarantees each element is used at most once. After all numbers are processed, dp[X] indicates whether the required sum is achievable. This runs in O(N·X) time and O(X) memory per test case, which easily satisfies the given limits.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();while(T-->0){st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int n=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int x=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());boolean[] dp=new boolean[x+1];dp[0]=true;st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int a=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());for(int s=x;s>=a;s--){if(dp[s-a])dp[s]=true;}}sb.append(dp[x]?"YES":"NO");if(T>0)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
